starter.audience: Trying verification on your machine before you wire it into CI.
starter.valueUnlock: Verify locally for free; upgrade when you want CI to block bad deploys.

individual.audience: Personal projects or a single engineer running verification in CI.
individual.valueUnlock: Run verification in CI with API access and included usage each month.

team.audience: Everyone on the team runs the same CI checks against the real database.
team.valueUnlock: Run verification across shared CI pipelines with room for normal team usage—without constantly hitting limits.

business.audience: Many services or heavy pipelines where you need predictable cost and headroom.
business.valueUnlock: Keep the same enforcement while scaling usage across busy pipelines and more services.

enterprise.audience: Procurement and invoicing, custom limits or SLAs, and on-prem or tightly controlled environments.
enterprise.valueUnlock: Sales-led onboarding, tailored limits, and contract terms outside self-serve checkout.
